description: Updates an existing release in a GitHub repository, allowing modification of its attributes; if linking a discussion, the `discussion_category_name` must refer to an existing category in the repository.

body.description: Text describing the contents of the release. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.
body.title: Body
examples: This release includes performance improvements and bug fixes related to user authentication.

name.description: The title of the release.
name.title: Name

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

draft.description: Set to `true` to mark the release as a draft, or `false` to publish it. Default: `false`.
draft.title: Draft

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

tag_name.description: The name of the tag. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.
tag_name.title: Tag Name

prerelease.description: Set to `true` to identify the release as a pre-release, or `false` for a full release. Default: `false`.
prerelease.title: Prerelease

release_id.description: The unique identifier of the release to be updated.
release_id.title: Release Id

make_latest.description: Specifies whether this release should be set as the latest release for the repository. Accepts 'true', 'false', or 'legacy'. Drafts and pre-releases cannot be set as latest. Defaults to `true` for newly published releases. `legacy` specifies that the latest release should be determined based on the release creation date and higher semantic version.
make_latest.title: Make Latest

target_commitish.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name or commit SHA) that determines where the Git tag is created from. Can be any branch or commit SHA. This parameter is unused if the Git tag specified by `tag_name` already exists. Defaults to the repository's default branch if omitted.
target_commitish.title: Target Commitish

discussion_category_name.description: If specified, a discussion of this category is created and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository. This parameter is ignored if a discussion is already linked to the release. For more information, see "[Managing categories for discussions in your repository](https://docs.github.com/discussions/managing-discussions-for-your-community/managing-categories-for-discussions-in-your-repository)."
discussion_category_name.title: Discussion Category Name

name: Update a repository
description: Updates settings for an existing GitHub repository, such as name, description, visibility, merge strategies, and security configurations.

name.description: New name for the repository (not case-sensitive).
name.title: Name

repo.description: ACTUAL name of an existing repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real repository that exists and is accessible - placeholder values like 'your-repo-name' will cause 404 errors.
repo.title: Repo

owner.description: ACTUAL GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real, existing account - placeholder values like 'your-username' will cause 404 errors.
owner.title: Owner

private.description: Whether the repository is private. IMPORTANT: Public forks cannot be made private - this is a GitHub platform restriction. Changing visibility may also be restricted by organization policies.
private.title: Private

archived.description: Archives the repository (making it read-only) or unarchives it.
archived.title: Archived

has_issues.description: Enables GitHub Issues for this repository.
has_issues.title: Has Issues

visibility.description: Repository visibility. IMPORTANT: Public forks cannot be made private - this is a GitHub platform restriction.
visibility.title: VisibilityEnm

description.description: Brief summary of the repository.
description.title: Description
examples: A library for advanced data processing.

is_template.description: Designates this repository as a template repository.
is_template.title: Is Template

has_projects.description: Enables GitHub Projects. Note: Organization settings might override this and cause an error if projects are disabled organization-wide.
has_projects.title: Has Projects

allow_forking.description: Allows private forks of this repository. IMPORTANT: This setting can ONLY be changed for organization-owned repositories. Attempting to set this for user-owned repositories will result in a 422 error. Omit this field if you don't want to change the forking setting.
allow_forking.title: Allow Forking

allow_merge_commit.description: Allows merging pull requests via merge commits.
allow_merge_commit.title: Allow Merge Commit

merge_commit_title.description: Default title for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'MERGE_MESSAGE'. CONSTRAINT: If set to 'PR_TITLE', merge_commit_message must be 'PR_BODY' or 'BLANK'. If set to 'MERGE_MESSAGE', merge_commit_message must be blank/unset.
merge_commit_title.title: MergeCommitTitleEnm

allow_update_branch.description: Allows updating a pull request's head branch from the base branch if not strictly required for merging.
allow_update_branch.title: Allow Update Branch

merge_commit_message.description: Default message for merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'PR_TITLE', or 'BLANK'. CONSTRAINT: If merge_commit_title is 'PR_TITLE', this must be 'PR_BODY' or 'BLANK'. Cannot be set when merge_commit_title is 'MERGE_MESSAGE'.
merge_commit_message.title: MergeCommitMessageEnm

delete_branch_on_merge.description: Automatically deletes head branches after merging pull requests.
delete_branch_on_merge.title: Delete Branch On Merge

squash_merge_commit_title.description: Default title for squash merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE'.
squash_merge_commit_title.title: SquashMergeCommitTitleEnm

squash_merge_commit_message.description: Default message for squash merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'COMMIT_MESSAGES', or 'BLANK'.
squash_merge_commit_message.title: SquashMergeCommitMessageEnm

web_commit_signoff_required.description: Requires contributors to sign off on web-based commits.
web_commit_signoff_required.title: Web Commit Signoff Required

use_squash_pr_title_as_default.description: DEPRECATED: Use `squash_merge_commit_title`. If true, squash merge commits use PR title; otherwise, commit message.

name: Update a team
description: Updates a team's settings (e.g., name, description, privacy, parent team) within a GitHub organization, identified by its slug and organization name.

org.description: The organization's name (case-insensitive). This is a required path parameter.
org.title: Org

name.description: New display name for the team.

privacy.description:
The desired privacy level for the team. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `privacy` intact. When a team is nested, the `privacy` for parent teams cannot be `secret`.
  - For a non-nested team:
    - `secret`: Visible only to organization owners and members of this team.
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).
  - For a parent or child team:
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).

team_slug.description: The team's unique slug identifier (typically URL-friendly version of team name). This is a required path parameter.

To remove an existing parent team relationship and make this a top-level team, explicitly pass `null` for this field (e.g., by setting this parameter to Python `None`). If this parameter is not provided in the request, the current parent team, if any, remains unchanged.
parent_team_id.title: Parent Team Id

notification_setting.description:
The notification setting the team has chosen. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `notification_setting` intact.
  - `notifications_enabled`: Team members receive notifications when the team is @mentioned.
  - `notifications_disabled`: No one receives notifications for team @mentions.

name: Update Project V2 Item for User
description: Tool to update a field value for an item in a user's GitHub ProjectV2. Use when you need to modify a field value of an existing project item.

value.description: The value to set. Use {'text': 'value'} for text fields, {'number': 123} for number fields, {'singleSelectOptionId': 'option_id'} for single select, {'date': '2024-01-01'} for date fields, {'iterationId': 'iteration_id'} for iteration fields.
value.title: Value

itemId.description: The global node ID of the project item to update (e.g., 'PVTI_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzgldG_w').
itemId.title: Item Id

fieldId.description: The global node ID of the field to update (e.g., 'PVTSSF_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzg8vTnA').
fieldId.title: Field Id

projectId.description: The global node ID of the Project (e.g., 'PVT_kwHOCNqc1s4BN3oq').
projectId.title: Project Id

name: Change User Status
description: Tool to update your status on GitHub. Use when you need to set or clear your GitHub profile status with an emoji, message, and availability indicator.

emoji.description: The emoji to represent your status. Can either be a native Unicode emoji or an emoji name with colons, e.g., :rocket:, :smiley:. If omitted, any existing emoji will be cleared.
emoji.title: Emoji

message.description: A short description of your current status. Maximum 80 characters. If omitted, any existing message will be cleared.
message.title: Message

expiresAt.description: If set, the user status will not be shown after this date. Must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g., 2024-12-31T23:59:59Z). If omitted, the status will persist indefinitely.
expiresAt.title: Expires At

organizationId.description: The global node ID of the organization whose members will be allowed to see the status. If omitted, the status will be publicly visible to everyone. Use this to limit status visibility to organization members only.
organizationId.title: Organization Id

clientMutationId.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This value will be returned in the response and can be used for request tracking or idempotency.
clientMutationId.title: Client Mutation Id

limitedAvailability.description: Whether this status should indicate you are not fully available on GitHub (e.g., you are away or busy). When true, shows a busy indicator. Defaults to false if omitted.
limitedAvailability.title: Limited Availability

inputParameters.title: UpdateUserStatusRequest
inputParameters.description:
Request parameters for updating the authenticated user's status on GitHub.

All parameters are optional. If no parameters are provided, the status will be cleared.

name: Upload a release asset
description: Tool to upload a binary asset to an existing GitHub release. Use when you need to attach files like installers or backups to a release programmatically.

repo.description: Repository name without the .git extension; not case sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

asset.description: Binary file to upload as the release asset.
asset.title: FileUploadable

name.description: The filename that will be used when uploading the file to the destination service
name.title: Name

s3key.description: The S3 key of a publicly accessible file, typically returned from a previous download action that stored the file in S3. This key references an existing file that can be uploaded to another service.
s3key.title: S3Key

mimetype.description: The MIME type of the file
mimetype.title: Mimetype

label.description: Short description for the asset; displayed instead of filename.
label.title: Label

owner.description: Account owner of the repository; not case sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

release_id.description: Unique identifier of the release. The release must have a valid upload URL (i.e., not already finalized without one). Asset size must not exceed 2 GB.
release_id.title: Release Id

content_type.description: Media type of the asset, for example 'application/zip'.
content_type.title: Content Type

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action
error.title: Error

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not
successful.title: Successful

name: Upload an analysis as sarif data
description: Uploads a gzipped and Base64 encoded SARIF file to a GitHub repository for a specific commit and reference; use `checkout_uri` if SARIF paths are absolute.

ref.description: The full Git reference for the analysis, formatted as `refs/heads/<branch name>`, `refs/tags/<tag>`, `refs/pull/<number>/merge`, or `refs/pull/<number>/head`.
ref.title: Ref

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.
repo.title: Repo

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This is case-insensitive.
owner.title: Owner

sarif.description: A Base64 encoded string of a gzipped SARIF file. For more information, see '[SARIF support for code scanning](https://docs.github.com/code-security/secure-coding/sarif-support-for-code-scanning)'.
sarif.title: Sarif
examples: eJzVst... (actual gzipped and Base64 encoded SARIF content)

validate.description: Specifies whether the uploaded SARIF file should be validated against the code scanning specifications. This is useful for integrators to ensure their SARIF files are correctly processed and rendered by GitHub.
validate.title: Validate

tool_name.description: Name of the tool that generated the analysis. GitHub defaults this to "API" if omitted. A tool GUID in the SARIF can be used for filtering alerts.
tool_name.title: Tool Name

commit_sha.description: The SHA of the commit to which the analysis you are uploading relates. Must be a 40-character Git SHA.
commit_sha.title: Commit Sha

started_at.description: The timestamp indicating when the analysis run began, in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.
started_at.title: Started At

checkout_uri.description: The base URI of the checkout directory as it appears in the SARIF file. This property is used to convert absolute file paths to relative paths, enabling alerts to be correctly mapped to their location in the repository.
checkout_uri.title: Checkout Uri

inputParameters.title: UploadSarifAnalysisRequest
inputParameters.description: Request schema for `UploadSarifAnalysis`

name: Create or update repository custom property values
description: Creates or updates up to 30 custom property values for a repository; custom properties must be predefined at the organization or repository level, and setting a property's value to `null` removes it.

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

properties.description: A list of custom property objects as a JSON string to create or update for the repository. Each object in the list must contain `property_name` (string identifying the custom property) and `value` (the value for the property, which can be a string, number, or a single-select option, matching the property's defined type). Example: '[{"property_name": "environment", "value": "production"}]'
properties.title: Properties
